channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Newsom wants to speed up a delayed rule to make California homes more fire resistant
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom will order state officials to advance implementation of a state rule aiming to make homes more fire resistant in a wind-driven wildfire after years of delay.
item.dc:creator: Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Conductor Daniel Barenboim says he's been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease
item.description: Conductor Daniel Barenboim says he has been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease. The 82-year-old issued a statement Thursday on Instagram. He has appeared more frail since 2022, canceling scheduled performances that included a new production of Wagner's Ring Cycle in Berlin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population infuriated the Arab world. It stunned American allies and other global powers and even flummoxed members of Trump’s own party. The reaction in Israel was starkly different.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel makes preparations
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Willie Nelson mural demolished in downtown Austin
item.description: A nearly decade-old Willie Nelson mural is being torn down in Austin as part of a demolition project.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Fentanyl with $3M estimated street value seized; Man in custody
item.description: Deputies arrested a North Carolina man after seizing nearly 7 pounds of fentanyl with a street value of $3 million.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Bright, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ sues Illinois, Chicago over ‘sanctuary city’ laws
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department sued the state of Illinois, the city of Chicago and local officials Thursday over laws creating a sanctuary city.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2nd victim dies after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility; suspect due in court Thursday
item.description: A second person has died after a gunman opened fire in a cosmetics facility in New Albany, Ohio, late on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video captures dramatic rescue of Florida man found unresponsive on spinning boat
item.description: An unresponsive Florida man was rescued from a runaway boat on Lake Dias in dramatic footage released by the Volusia Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas convict issues last message to wife before execution for killing pastor
item.description: Texas man Steven Nelson, convicted in 2012 of murdering a pastor the year before, was put to death by lethal injection Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy shares conditions for ending Ukraine-Russia war and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA, NTSB to brief senators on Washington, DC midair collision
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration and National Transportation Safety Board are planning to brief senators just one week after a midair collision claimed the lives of 67 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boy Scouts see a small membership uptick after rebrand to Scouting America
item.description: A historic rebrand of the Boy Scouts of America has been followed by a small uptick in young people joining what will now be called Scouting America.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border following deal made to pause Trump-approved tariffs
item.description: Mexico has started deploying soldiers to its northern border in accordance with the 10,000 troops it promised to send to the border under a deal made with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about what's tying up a $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's Supreme Court is set to hear arguments Thursday over insurance issues that are tying up a potential $4 billion settlement over Maui's catastrophic 2023 wildfire, the deadliest in the U.S. in more than a century.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect arrested after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility leaves 1 dead, 5 injured
item.description: Officials announced that Foster had been arrested by U.S. Marshalls after a manhunt that stretched through the night.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, Fox 5

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in cities across the country
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mmm, that looks yummy! The colors we see make a difference in the food we eat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — You know you've said it. We all have. “Mmm, that looks so delicious — I want to try some!” That's because when it comes to what we eat, it's not just a matter of taste.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China hits U.S. with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Treasury tells Congress that DOGE has ‘Read Only’ access to payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Treasury Department official wrote a letter Tuesday to federal lawmakers saying that a tech executive working with Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency will have “read-only access” to the government's payment system.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN & JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims of the midair collision near DC recovered as NTSB probes altitude data
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — The remains of all 67 victims of last week's midair collision of an American Airlines flight and an Army helicopter near the nation's capital have been recovered, authorities said Tuesday. All but one has been identified.
item.dc:creator: SERKAN GURBUZ and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: The man charged with stabbing Salman Rushdie is going on trial. The author will take the stand
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — In 2022, Salman Rushdie was about to deliver a lecture before a live audience in western New York when a man ran towards him and plunged a knife into the author’s hand as he raised it in self-defense.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
